title: microlepia scoparia
intro: Microlepia scoparia, also known as Scoparia or Japanese mullein, is a type of flowering plant in the fern family (Polypodiaceae) native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Microlepia scoparia include its potential to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce stress. Microlepia scoparia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder for use in herbal remedies, and its leaves can also be used fresh in salads or as a garnish.
